source_stig.title: The F5 BIG-IP appliance must be configured to prohibit or restrict the use of unnecessary or prohibited functions, ports, protocols, and/or services, including those defined in the PPSM CAL and vulnerability assessments.
source_stig.checktext:
From the BIG-IP GUI:
1. Local Traffic.
2. Virtual Servers.
3. Verify the list of virtual servers are not configured to listen on unnecessary and/or nonsecure functions, ports, protocols, and/or services.

If any services are running that must not be, this is a finding.
source_stig.fixtext:
Check the PPSM CAL and the site's System Security Plan/documentation for a list of prohibited ports, protocols, and services.

From the BIG-IP GUI:
1. Local Traffic.
2. Virtual Servers.
3. For any virtual server(s) listening on all unnecessary and/or nonsecure functions, ports, protocols, and/or services, check the box next to the virtual server and click "Delete".
4. Click "Delete" again.
source_stig.description:
To prevent unauthorized connection of devices, unauthorized transfer of information, or unauthorized tunneling (i.e., embedding of data types within data types), organizations must disable or restrict unused or unnecessary physical and logical ports/protocols on information systems.

ALGs are capable of providing a wide variety of functions and services. Some of the functions and services provided by default may not be necessary to support essential organizational operations. DOD continually assesses the ports, protocols, and services that can be used for network communications. Some ports, protocols or services have known exploits or security weaknesses. Network traffic using these ports, protocols, and services must be prohibited or restricted in accordance with DOD policy. The ALG is a key network element for preventing these noncompliant ports, protocols, and services from causing harm to DOD information systems.

The network ALG must be configured to prevent or restrict the use of prohibited ports, protocols, and services throughout the network by filtering the network traffic and disallowing or redirecting traffic as necessary. Default and updated policy filters from the vendors will disallow older version of protocols and applications and will address most known nonsecure ports, protocols, and/or services. However, sources for further policy filters are the IAVMs and the PPSM requirements.

Satisfies: SRG-NET-000132-ALG-000087, SRG-NET-000131-ALG-000085

tmsh_ltm_virtual.preview:
ltm virtual stig_disallowed_test {
    address-status yes
    app-cloud-security-services none
    app-service none
    auth none
    auto-discovery disabled
    auto-lasthop default
    bwc-policy none
    clone-pools none
    cmp-enabled  ... (3977 more chars)

comparison_df.note: DISA check/fix text requires PPSM CAL and SSP/site documentation before virtual-server services can be judged.

pullback_df.finding: 1 measurement(s) cannot be closed by on-appliance evidence alone; external attestation is required.

human_review_row.requirement: The F5 BIG-IP appliance must be configured to prohibit or restrict the use of unnecessary or prohibited functions, ports, protocols, and/or services, including those defined in the PPSM CAL and vulnerability assessments.
human_review_row.why: Virtual-server ports/protocols/services require local PPSM CAL and SSP authorization evidence; no local allowlist was provided.
human_review_row.tmsh_remediation_command: [Manual] Disable or delete only the exact virtual servers that violate the local PPSM/SSP allowlist.
human_review_row.rest_remediation_command: [Manual] DELETE /mgmt/tm/ltm/virtual/<name> only after local authorization review.

violations: Virtual-server ports/protocols/services require local PPSM CAL and SSP authorization evidence; no local allowlist was provided.
